name: The Transformation Letter — Brand Style v2 (wadibardawil.com)
voice: advisor not pundit, diagnostic not promotional, restrained not loud

paletteNotes.background: dark charcoal — page background, header, primary dark surface
paletteNotes.primary: same as background — dark charcoal, never replaced with another color
paletteNotes.surface: elevated card / surface — slightly lifted off background
paletteNotes.foreground: primary text on dark — pure white
paletteNotes.accent: orange — CTAs and highlights ONLY. Never decorative.

typography.display: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, "Segoe UI", Roboto, "Helvetica Neue", Arial, sans-serif
typography.body: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, "Segoe UI", Roboto, "Helvetica Neue", Arial, sans-serif
typography.mono: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, "Liberation Mono", monospace

imageStyle.approach: editorial photography or restrained illustration on dark backgrounds; executive aesthetic
constraints: no realistic photography of identifiable people unless cleared
no stock-photo executives in suits or boardrooms
no AI tropes (robot hands, glowing brains, circuit boards, futuristic UI panels)
no clichéd transformation imagery (caterpillar/butterfly, sunrise/sunset, ladder, mountaintop, lightbulb)
no text or words in the image — typography is added in layout, not generated
no flags, logos, brand marks, or trademarked imagery
no decorative gradients — flat composition only
no light backgrounds — imagery must read on the dark charcoal canvas
preferences: abstract geometric forms suggesting structure, sequence, and process
architectural metaphors: foundations, scaffolding, blueprints, load-bearing walls
limited palette: charcoals + white + orange highlight (used sparingly, only when the photo subject demands it)
negative space as a deliberate composition element
matte finish; no shiny gradients, no neon, no chrome

captionVoice.guidance: One sentence. Anchors the image to the issue's diagnostic — not a description of the picture. Same voice as the letter: advisor, present tense, second-person where natural.
examplesOk: The structure your team needs is not a chart — it is the artifact you have not written yet.
Decision rights are scaffolding. Without them, every floor of the business rests on you.
examplesAvoid: An abstract illustration of business transformation.
Photo of business strategy and AI working together.

publisher.name: Wadi Bardawil
publisher.positioningTagline: I build the intelligence layer between your decisions and your operations.
publisher.location: Monterrey, Mexico — serving Mexico & United States
